incident_summary.title: A resource of type EKSCluster has executed a suspicious command.

decision_questions: 승인 전 EKS 클러스터 GeneratedFindingEKSClusterName 및 연관 워크로드 GeneratedFindingKubernetesWorkloadName 에 대한 운영 중단 허용 범위를 확인할까요?
원격 통신 차단을 위해 IP 198.51.100.1 차단이 업무상 허용되는지 확인할까요?
VPC vpc-12345678 에 대한 추가 모니터링 활성화가 가능한지 확인할까요?
escalation_assessment.approval_notes: Level 1 조치는 이미 수행되었습니다. 본 권고는 승인형 Level 2 및 Level 3 후보 플레이북이며, EKSCluster 리소스에 한정해 비-EC2 조치만 포함합니다.

reasoning_bullets: GuardDuty RuntimeMonitoring finding indicates a suspicious command executed in an EKSCluster workload, so additional containment and monitoring beyond Level 1 are warranted.
The incident resource type is EKSCluster, therefore EC2-specific actions such as isolate_instance, stop_instance, and create_snapshot are excluded.

Regulatory context emphasizes network restriction, segmentation, monitoring, alerting, and audit response, supporting progressive escalation from monitoring-focused Level 2 to stronger network containment at Level 3.
A VPC ID is present for the EKS cluster (vpc-12345678), so VPC flow log enablement can be recommended.

regulations.excerpt: Monitor, encrypt and restrict communications between environments to only authenticated and authorized connections, as justified by the business.
regulations.why_relevant: The suspicious command execution in the EKS cluster may involve unauthorized network communication, so restricting communications and monitoring traffic is directly relevant.

regulations.excerpt: Design, develop, deploy and configure applications and infrastructures such that CSP and CSC (tenant) user access and intra-tenant access is appropriately segmented and segregated, monitored and restricted from other tenants.
regulations.why_relevant: The finding suggests possible lateral movement or misuse within the cluster environment, making segmentation and restricted access relevant for containment.

regulations.excerpt: Audit encryption and key management systems, policies, and processes with a frequency that is proportional to the risk exposure of the system with audit occurring preferably continuously but at least annually and after any security event(s).
regulations.why_relevant: Because this is a security event, post-incident compliance and audit review are relevant even though the finding is not primarily a KMS event.

regulations.excerpt: Identify and monitor security-related events within applications and the underlying infrastructure. Define and implement a system to generate alerts to responsible stakeholders based on such events and corresponding metrics.
regulations.why_relevant: The suspicious runtime command in the EKS workload requires enhanced monitoring, alerting, and stakeholder notification to support timely response.

regulations.excerpt: Monitor security audit logs to detect activity outside of typical or expected patterns. Establish and follow a defined process to review and take appropriate and timely actions on detected anomalies.
regulations.why_relevant: This finding is an anomalous runtime activity and supports log review, triage, and documented response actions.

recommended_actions.description: Lower-impact response for the suspicious EKS runtime event focused on network visibility and alerting before stronger containment.

selected_playbook.description: Lower-impact response for the suspicious EKS runtime event focused on network visibility and alerting before stronger containment.

alternative_playbooks.description: Higher-impact response for the suspicious EKS runtime event using network containment and additional monitoring to reduce ongoing exposure.
alternative_playbooks.why_not_selected: Not selected as primary response; router committed to L2. (High severity (8.0) but no L3 triggers → prefer L2.)

route_reasons: High severity (8.0) but no L3 triggers → prefer L2.
